arguments.task: Research GoHighLevel using official first-party pages. Visit the GoHighLevel API docs at highlevel.stoplight.io and official help/pricing pages. Extract: one-line product description, API authentication methods, whether access is self-serve or gated and why, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), breadth of API, any official rate-limit notes, and evidence URLs. Also check whether there is an official MCP server or an official GitHub/MCP registry entry for GoHighLevel. Return only findings supported by the pages you visited, with exact URLs.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official GoHighLevel documentation and pages for verified research evidence.

raw_result.description: All-in-one marketing, CRM, automation, and communications platform for agencies and businesses.
raw_result.auth_notes: The developer portal shows 'API 2.0 Docs (OAuth based)' and also references legacy API 1.0 docs; the exact legacy auth mechanism was not verified from the gathered evidence, so 'other' is used for the older API and needs review.
raw_result.gating_reason: Pricing evidence gathered during browsing indicated paid plans such as Starter ($97) and Unlimited ($297), with the latter mentioning 'Basic API Access'; this suggests API access requires a paid subscription.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer portal exists with separate API 2.0 and API 1.0 documentation, indicating a meaningful public API surface, but endpoint breadth was not fully verified from the gathered pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or registry entry was verified from the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Public developer docs were found, but the initially provided docs URL was invalid and the exact auth details, rate limits, and MCP status were not fully verified from completed page extraction.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit documentation was not verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: The provided Stoplight hint URL appears invalid ('Workspace does not exist'). The official developer portal at developers.gohighlevel.com appears to be the authoritative source and exposes API 2.0 (OAuth-based) plus legacy API 1.0 docs. Pricing evidence observed during browsing referenced paid tiers and 'Basic API Access' on Unlimited.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several required fields remain only partially supported by the gathered evidence: the exact auth method for legacy API 1.0, precise access gating for API on each plan, rate limits, endpoint breadth, and whether an MCP exists. The browser run also did not finish with a final extracted output.
